design_rationale: Globtier is a dealer intelligence and performance management platform designed to replace Excel dashboards. Thus, the interface must prioritize high density, stark contrast, and unquestionable clarity. The Swiss archetype is chosen to give a sterile, authoritative foundation (10% grey/stark white) where data flags (Green, Amber, Red) can pop instantly. Using 'F-Pattern' hierarchy and data-dense tabular designs, the UI supports robust operations, metrics monitoring, and regional comparisons.

tables.style: Zebra striping optional, sticky headers, right-aligned numbers for financials, borders between rows, dense padding.

types: KPI Radar Chart (for ROS, OAR, SSI, DCSI, KDEP)
Monthly Performance Bar Chart (Target vs Achievement)

cards.style: Flat, 1px subtle border, sharp or slightly rounded corners, white background. Hover slightly lifts and darkens border.

indicators.flags: Use badges (Green/Amber/Red) for Performance Flags. High contrast text on light background.

login_bg.description: Abstract geometric dark blue and corporate white data nodes background, used for login page.

user_portrait.description: Professional business woman in black blazer, used for user avatar in header/sidebar.

workshop_1.description: Auto workshop interior with car being worked on. Use as sample media in infrastructure module.

showroom_1.description: Modern car dealership showroom. Use as sample media in infrastructure module.

company_logo.description: Company logo (Konica minolta proxy) to simulate Globtier logo where SVG is not used.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the dashboard focusing on dense, tabular data to replace the existing Excel-based workflows.
Implement Recharts for Sales Trends (line chart), Monthly Performance (bar chart), and KPIs (Radar chart).
Ensure performance flags (Green/Amber/Red) strictly follow the state colors and are clearly visible as badges or cell backgrounds in the table.
Use standard Shadcn UI components but constrain their padding to match the 'comfortable-dense' specs (e.g., h-9 inputs).
In the Group Dashboard view, include a data table for outlet-level comparison, complete with sortable headers.
Incorporate the provided media URLs for user avatars and placeholder images for the Showroom/Workshop media upload section.
